1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: true
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am mandatory in the review phase. Dispatch one checker per .lean
11	    file that received prover work this iteration — every file, no
12	    exceptions. Skipping any prover-touched file is a review-phase
13	    failure, not editorial discretion. Each dispatch is independent
14	    and parallel-able.
15	  - Also dispatch on any file the lean-auditor flagged as suspicious,
16	    even if it received no prover work this iter.
17	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
18	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
19	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
20	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
21	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide formalization,
22	    missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly needed), not
23	    only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate, the plan agent
24	    should dispatch a blueprint-writer to address it.
25	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
26	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
27	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
28	---
29	
30	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
31	
32	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
33	
34	## Your Job
35	
36	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
37	
38	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
39	
40	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
41	
42	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
43	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
44	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
45	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
46	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
47	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
48	
49	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
50	
51	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
52	
53	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
54	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
55	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
56	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
57	
58	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
59	
60	## Directive Format
61	
62	```markdown
63	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
64	
65	## Slug
66	<slug>
67	
68	## Lean file
69	<path/to/file.lean>
70	
71	## Blueprint chapter
72	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
73	
74	## Known issues
75	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
76	```
77	
78	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the lean-auditor's and blueprint-reviewer's job; you stay focused on this one pair.
79	
80	## What you do
81	
82	1. Read your directive.
83	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
84	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
85	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
86	   - It exists.
87	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
88	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
89	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
90	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
91	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
92	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
93	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
94	   - `axiom` declarations.
95	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
96	
97	You may use:
98	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
99	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
100	
101	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
102	
103	## Report format
104	
105	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
106	
107	```markdown
108	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
109	
110	## Slug
111	<slug>
112	
113	## Iteration
114	<NNN>
115	
116	## Files audited
117	- Lean: <path>
118	- Blueprint: <path>
119	
120	## Per-declaration
121	
122	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
123	
124	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
125	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
126	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
127	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
128	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
129	
130	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
131	
132	## Red flags
133	
134	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
135	
136	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
137	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
138	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
139	- ...
140	
141	### Excuse-comments
142	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
143	- ...
144	
145	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
146	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
147	
148	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
149	
150	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
151	
152	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
153	
154	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
155	
156	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
157	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
158	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
159	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
160	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writer should land if you flagged anything above>
161	
162	## Severity summary
163	
164	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
167	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
168	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
169	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
170	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
171	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
172	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
173	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
174	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
175	
176	Overall verdict: one sentence.
177	```
178	
179	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and blueprint-reviewer's dispatcher_notes) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
180	
181	## Return value
182	
183	Your final assistant message:
184	
185	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
186	- The path to your full report.
187	
188	## Reminders
189	
190	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to lean-auditor and blueprint-reviewer.
191	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
192	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
193	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
194

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
10	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
12	
13	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
14	
15	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
16	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
17	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
18	`Ω` on affine opens.
19	
20	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
21	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
22	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
23	
24	## References
25	
26	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
38	
39	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
40	
41	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
42	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
43	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
44	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
45	
46	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
47	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
48	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
49	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
50	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
51	    f.c
52	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
53	
54	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
55	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
56	Kähler differential module of the ring map
57	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
58	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
59	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
60	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
61	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
62	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
63	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
64	  rfl
65	
66	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
67	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
68	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
69	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
70	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
71	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
72	
73	The bridge from this algebra-Kähler form to the project's presheaf
74	form `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)` is a
75	Mathlib gap: the section module of the presheaf identifies with the
76	Kähler module over the inverse-image-presheaf colimit ring
77	`colim_{f V ⊆ W ⊆ S} Γ(S, W)`, which is a localization of `Γ(S, U)`
78	and hence yields an iso `Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ≃ Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ A_colim]`
79	via `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`; constructing the iso
80	requires presheaf-level cofinality machinery (~200–400 LOC) that is
81	out of autonomous-loop scope. See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`
82	§ "Bridge to the relative cotangent presheaf — out of autonomous-loop
83	scope" for the mathematical content.
84	
85	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
86	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
87	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
88	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
89	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
90	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
91	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
92	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
93	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
94	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
95	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
96	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
97	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
98	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
99	  intro x
100	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
101	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
102	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
103	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
104	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
105	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
106	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
107	      first
108	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
109	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
110	
111	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
112

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$, where $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}$ denotes the \emph{presheaf-level} inverse-image functor (left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$).
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies that on every affine chart $V \to U$ (with $V \subseteq X$, $U \subseteq S$ affine and $f V \subseteq U$) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The relationship between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ is the content of milestone~M1 of the project roadmap and is stated as Theorem~\ref{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE} in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The presheaf-level inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S}$ on $X$ (the left Kan extension of $\struct{S}$ along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$, given on each open $V \subseteq X$ by the colimit $\mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$) is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) \,/\, (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  Here $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the presheaf-level inverse-image section ring, \emph{not} $\struct{S}(U)$ for an arbitrary affine open $U \supseteq f V$. The relationship between this colimit-source K\"ahler module and the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$ for $A = \struct{S}(U)$, $B = \struct{X}(V)$ on an affine chart $V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}U$ with $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$ affine is the content of the bridge documented in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The colimit description of $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$ is the definition of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} as the left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$ in Mathlib.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$, not for $\struct{S}(U)$. Transferring to the classical $A = \struct{S}(U)$ source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of $X$ admits an affine chart $V \to U$ over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of the prescribed rank, with $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
53	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exist an affine open $U \subseteq S$, an affine open $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)$, such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure $\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)$ induced by $f$) is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$.
58	\end{theorem}

108	\section{Bridge: presheaf form $\leftrightarrow$ algebra-K\"ahler form on an affine chart (milestone M1)}
109	\label{sec:bridge}
110	
111	Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} is stated in the \emph{algebra-K\"ahler form} $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) / \Gamma(S, U)}$ for an affine chart $V \subseteq f^{-1}U$, while the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ of Definition~\ref{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf} carries on its section over $V$ the K\"ahler module $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) / A_{\mathrm{colim}}}$ over the presheaf-level inverse-image colimit ring $A_{\mathrm{colim}} = (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$. On a single affine chart the two are connected by a canonical $B$-linear isomorphism whose construction is the content of milestone~M1 of the project roadmap (\texttt{STRATEGY.md}).
112	
113	\begin{theorem}[Bridge between presheaf and algebra-K\"ahler forms]
114	  \label{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE}
115	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE}
116	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj, lem:appLE_isLocalization}
117	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes, let $U \subseteq S$ and $V \subseteq X$ be affine opens with $V \leq f^{-1}U$, and write $A := \Gamma(S, U)$, $B := \Gamma(X, V)$, with the appLE algebra structure $A \to B$ induced by $f$. There is a canonical $B$-linear isomorphism
118	  \[
119	    (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(V)
120	    \;\cong_{B}\;
121	    \Omega_{B \,/\, A}
122	  \]
123	  identifying the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf on $V$ with the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential module of Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}.
124	\end{theorem}
125	
126	\begin{proof}
127	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj, lem:appLE_isLocalization, lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton, lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
128	  The proof decomposes into the four sub-steps M1.a--M1.e of the roadmap. We use the abbreviation $A_{\mathrm{colim}} := (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ for the colimit ring carrying the presheaf-side K\"ahler module.
129	
130	  \emph{M1.a: the multiplicative set of ``good'' elements.} Define
131	  \[
132	    M \;:=\; \{\, g \in A \,:\, (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times} \,\}
133	  \]
134	  as the set of elements of $A$ whose image in $B$ is a unit. $M$ is a submonoid: $1 \in M$ (the appLE map is a ring homomorphism, so $\mathrm{appLE}(1) = 1$, a unit), and if $g, h \in M$ then $\mathrm{appLE}(gh) = \mathrm{appLE}(g)\,\mathrm{appLE}(h) \in B^{\times}$ as the product of two units.
135	
136	  \emph{M1.b: $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ is the localization at $M$.} This is the heart of the bridge and is the standalone Mathlib contribution candidate; see Lemma~\ref{lem:appLE_isLocalization} below for the statement. The argument: the directed system $\{W \subseteq S : f V \subseteq W\}$ admits a cofinal subsystem of basic opens $D(g) \subseteq U$ with $g \in M$ (these are exactly the affine opens between $f V$ and $U$ on which $g$ becomes a unit after pulling back to $B$, by $V \subseteq f^{-1}D(g) \iff \mathrm{appLE}(g) \in B^{\times}$); for each such $g$, the section ring $\struct{S}(D(g)) = A_{g}$ is the localization of $A$ at $g$ (Mathlib's \texttt{IsAffineOpen.basicOpen\_isLocalization}); and the directed colimit of these localizations over $M$ is the localization $A_{M}$ of $A$ at the full multiplicative set $M$.
137	
138	  \emph{M1.c: the K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial.} Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton} below: if $A \to L$ is a localization at a multiplicative subset $M \subseteq A$, then $\Omega_{L/A}$ is subsingleton (equivalently, zero as an $L$-module). Reason: every element of $L$ is of the form $a / g$ with $a \in A$, $g \in M$, and $\mathrm{d}(a/g) = (\mathrm{d}a - (a/g)\,\mathrm{d}g)/g = 0$ in $\Omega_{L/A}$ (both $\mathrm{d}a$ and $\mathrm{d}g$ vanish, being images of elements of $A$ under a derivation over $A$); since $L$ is generated by such elements and $\mathrm{d}$ is a universal derivation, the whole module is zero. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has \emph{no off-the-shelf} bare lemma for ``$\Omega_{L/A}$ is subsingleton when $A \to L$ is a localization''; the closest is \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelationDimension.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential} (which requires the much stronger \texttt{IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S}). This M1.c sub-lemma is therefore a genuine Mathlib gap-fill: the proof is short (10--30 LOC), uses the universal property of the K\"ahler differential module plus the fact that $\mathrm{d}$ kills units (via the formula $\mathrm{d}(g g^{-1}) = 0 = g \,\mathrm{d}(g^{-1}) + g^{-1}\,\mathrm{d}g$, so $\mathrm{d}(g^{-1}) = -g^{-2}\,\mathrm{d}g$, which is zero when $g \in A$). Candidate Mathlib home: \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}.
139	
140	  \emph{M1.d: the surjection $\Omega_{B/A} \twoheadrightarrow \Omega_{B/A_{\mathrm{colim}}}$ is an iso.} Apply Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso} below (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map} in Mathlib \texttt{RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler}, plus the M1.c conclusion that $\Omega_{A_{\mathrm{colim}}/A} = 0$). The second fundamental exact sequence
141	  \[
142	    B \otimes_{A_{\mathrm{colim}}} \Omega_{A_{\mathrm{colim}} / A}
143	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B / A}
144	    \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B / A_{\mathrm{colim}}}
145	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
146	  \]
147	  has zero left-hand term by M1.c, so the right-hand surjection is an iso.
148	
149	  \emph{M1.e: combine with the $\mathrm{rfl}$-identification of the presheaf section.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, $(\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(V) = \Omega_{B / A_{\mathrm{colim}}}$ definitionally. Composing the rfl-equality with the inverse of the M1.d iso gives the claimed $B$-linear isomorphism.
150	\end{proof}

152	\begin{lemma}[$A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ is a localization at $M$]
153	  \label{lem:appLE_isLocalization}
154	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.appLE_isLocalization}
155	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
156	  In the setting of Theorem~\ref{thm:relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE}, the canonical ring homomorphism $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ exhibits $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ as the localization of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M := \{g \in A : (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times}\}$.
157	\end{lemma}
158	
159	\begin{proof}
160	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
161	  The proof decomposes into four steps that together identify $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ with the localization $A_M$ of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M$.
162	
163	  \emph{Step 1: cofinality of basic opens.} The directed system $\{W \subseteq S : f V \subseteq W \subseteq U\}$ of opens between $f V$ and $U$ admits as a cofinal subfamily the family of basic opens
164	  \[
165	    \{D(g) \subseteq U : g \in A,\ f V \subseteq D(g)\} \;=\; \{D(g) : g \in M\}.
166	  \]
167	  Cofinality holds because: (i) every open $W \subseteq U$ of the affine scheme $U = \Spec A$ is a union of basic opens $D(g_i)$ (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Spec}, via \texttt{PrimeSpectrum.isBasis\_basic\_opens} or equivalently \texttt{Opens.exists\_finset\_inf\_le\_basicOpen}); (ii) for any $W$ with $f V \subseteq W \subseteq U$, the topological inclusion $f V \subseteq W = \bigcup_i D(g_i)$ combined with quasi-compactness of $f V$ (the image of the quasi-compact affine $V \subseteq X$ under the continuous map $f$, contained in finitely many of the $D(g_i)$) selects a finite sub-cover $f V \subseteq D(g_{i_1}) \cup \cdots \cup D(g_{i_n})$; (iii) for the product $g := g_{i_1} \cdots g_{i_n}$ one has $D(g) = D(g_{i_1}) \cap \cdots \cap D(g_{i_n})$, hence $D(g) \subseteq D(g_{i_j}) \subseteq W$ for each $j$ and $f V \subseteq D(g)$, witnessing the cofinal refinement.
168	
169	  The bridge identity is
170	  \[
171	    f V \subseteq D(g) \;\iff\; (f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}(g) \in B^{\times}, \qquad \text{i.e.\ } g \in M.
172	  \]
173	  This follows from \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.preimage\_basicOpen\_eq\_basicOpen} together with the characterisation ``$V \subseteq X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,h$ iff $h$ is a unit in $\Gamma(X, V)$'' (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.basicOpen\_eq\_iff\_isUnit}, or the closely related \texttt{Scheme.basicOpen\_eq\_of\_isUnit}).
174	
175	  \emph{Step 2: each $\struct{S}(D(g))$ is the single-element localization $A_g$.} For every $g \in M$, the basic-open section ring is $\struct{S}(D(g)) = A_g$, with structure map the localization of $A$ at the powers of $g$. Mathlib leverage: \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_basicOpen}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}) supplies the instance \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$(\Gamma(S, X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,g))$, i.e.\ localization of $A$ at the single element $g$.
176	
177	  \emph{Step 3: directed colimit of single-element localizations is localization at the full submonoid.} The directed colimit
178	  \[
179	    \operatorname*{colim}_{g \in M}\,A_g
180	  \]
181	  of single-element localizations indexed by the (filtered) submonoid $M$ (with transition maps $A_g \to A_{gh}$ inverting the new factor $h$) is the localization $\mathrm{Localization}\,M$ of $A$ at the multiplicative set $M$. This is the classical fact that localization at a multiplicative set is the directed colimit of localizations at the finitely-generated sub-monoids it contains, applied to the principal generating sub-monoids $\langle g \rangle = \{g^n\}_{n \geq 0}$ for $g \in M$. Mathlib leverage: \texttt{IsLocalization.Away}~$g$~$A_g$ for each individual node, plus the universal-property characterisation via \texttt{IsLocalization.map} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic}). The exact name of the lemma ``colimit of single-element localizations under inclusion of generated submonoids equals the localization at the full submonoid'' may not yet exist in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} in this packaged form; if missing it is a Mathlib gap to be filled as part of M1.b. Candidate Mathlib home: \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Submonoid} (general filtered-submonoid colimit) or by analogy with the pattern in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.AtPrime}.
182	
183	  \emph{Step 4: identify the colimit with $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.} The project's $A_{\mathrm{colim}} := (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W :\, f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the colimit over the directed system of opens containing $f V$. Restricting to the sub-cone of opens $W \subseteq U$ does not change the colimit (any $W$ containing $f V$ can be intersected with $U$ without altering the cone since $f V \subseteq U$). By the cofinality of Step 1, this colimit equals the colimit over the cofinal subfamily $\{D(g) : g \in M\}$; by Step 2 the nodes of that subfamily are the $A_g$; by Step 3 the colimit over that subfamily is $\mathrm{Localization}\,M$. Therefore the canonical algebra map $A \to A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ exhibits $A_{\mathrm{colim}}$ as the localization of $A$ at $M$, i.e.\ \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$A_{\mathrm{colim}}$.
184	
185	  \emph{Mathlib-gap summary.} The cofinality lemma ``colimit over a cofinal subsystem equals colimit over the full system'' exists in Mathlib as the general \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.Final} machinery (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Final}); its application to \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} may need a wrapper. Candidate Mathlib home for that wrapper: \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Presheaf.InverseImage} (new file) or \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Cofinality}. The topological analogue of the cofinality argument is recorded as Stacks Tag~02M5.
186	
187	  \emph{Cross-chapter parallel.} The same cofinality argument has a parallel in M2.a's $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb{P}^{1}_{k}, A) = A(k)$ rigidity step (see \texttt{Jacobian.tex} \S~C.2), where the bridge between the algebraic-side $\Spec$ and the scheme-side $\mathbb{P}^{1}$ proceeds via a similar localization-cofinality unfold.
188	
189	  The Mathlib analogist's persistent file \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md} (this iter) records the design rationale for the chosen API shape.
190	\end{proof}

192	\begin{lemma}[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
193	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
194	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
195	  \uses{}
196	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization at a multiplicative subset $M \subseteq A$. Then $\Omega_{L / A}$ is subsingleton (the zero $L$-module).
197	\end{lemma}
198	
199	\begin{proof}
200	  Every element of $L$ is of the form $\iota(a)\,\iota(g)^{-1}$ for some $a \in A$ and $g \in M$ (where $\iota \colon A \to L$ is the structure map). In $\Omega_{L/A}$, the universal derivation $\mathrm{d}$ over $A$ kills $\iota(a)$ and $\iota(g)$ (both are images of $A$-elements). Therefore $\mathrm{d}(\iota(a)\,\iota(g)^{-1}) = 0$ via the quotient rule, and the image of $\mathrm{d} \colon L \to \Omega_{L/A}$ is zero. Since $\Omega_{L/A}$ is generated as an $L$-module by $\mathrm{d}(L)$ (the universal property), the whole module is zero. Mathlib's \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton\_of\_isLocalization} packages this conclusion when available; otherwise the proof is a direct application of the universal property of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} as a quotient of $L \otimes_{\mathbb Z} L$ modulo the Leibniz / $A$-linearity relations.
201	\end{proof}

215	  \]
216	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}, the left term vanishes. Therefore the right surjection has zero kernel, i.e.\ is a $B$-linear iso. Mathlib's \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler}) packages the same conclusion via the surjection-with-zero-kernel argument; in the project we may either reuse this lemma directly (preferred) or write the second-fundamental-sequence argument inline if the Mathlib name is not in the pinned snapshot.

228	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
229	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
230	
231	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
232	
233	The reverse implication
234	\[
235	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
236	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
237	\]
238	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
239	
240	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
241	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
242	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
243	\end{remark}
244	
245	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
246	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
247	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
248	  \[
249	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
250	  \]
251	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
252	  \begin{itemize}
253	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
254	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
255	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
256	  \end{itemize}
257	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
258	\end{remark}
259	
260	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
261	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
262	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2).
